key_insight: PASS is a middleware that fixes three GRPO process-supervision failure modes—signal contamination, credit-resolution mismatch, and length-driven bias—so step-level process signals can be used more cleanly for LLM reasoning RL.
engineering_relevance: It provides a plug-in way to improve process-supervised reinforcement learning without changing the underlying GRPO trainer, making it easier to deploy across different reward signals and task domains.
method: Advantage Fusion: standardize process, outcome, and format signals separately within each group.
Chunk-by-Value: form value-homogeneous chunks from the signal and share credit within each chunk.
Divide-Length: replace cumulative return with average value density to reduce length bias.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent pass@1 improvements over GRPO across two domains, two signal paradigms (PRM and on-policy-distillation KL), and two group-standardization operators.
watch_item: The chunking and normalization steps add design choices that may be sensitive to signal quality, chunk boundaries, and implementation details in different training setups.
paper_title: Process Advantage Signal Shaping: A Paradigm-Agnostic Middleware for Process-Supervised RL in LLM Reasoners
